items.title: Match transforms to label semantics
items.avoid: Copying ImageNet augmentation recipes for medical text, OCR, or left/right classification tasks.
items.prefer: List invariances the label should ignore; enable only those transforms; spot-check augmented samples manually.
items.why: Label-preserving is the core contract — semantic mistakes are worse than no augmentation.
items.example: Disable vertical flip for aerial images where "north" matters for crop type labels.

items.title: Separate train and eval preprocessing
items.prefer: Random transforms on train; fixed Resize + CenterCrop (or identity) on val/test.
items.why: Stable held-out metrics reflect deployment inputs and reveal overfitting honestly.
items.example: train: RandomResizedCrop; val: Resize(256) + CenterCrop(224) — same spatial size, no randomness.

items.title: Start mild, tune on validation
items.avoid: Maximum color jitter and cutout on day one without a baseline.
items.prefer: Train a no-augmentation baseline; add one transform group at a time; track val metric.
items.why: Incremental tuning shows which diversity helps vs hurts for your dataset.
items.example: Val accuracy +3% after flip+crop only; extra rotation hurts — drop rotation.

items.title: Keep synthetic oversampling inside training
items.avoid: SMOTE resampling the full dataset before splitting.
items.prefer: Split → SMOTE on train only → evaluate on real val/test rows.
items.why: Prevents synthetic leakage and keeps test scores trustworthy.
items.example: imblearn Pipeline: SMOTE step inside CV train fold only via imblearn.pipeline.
